input.command: curl -s 'http://localhost:9274/ax-db-orders/_mapping' | python3 -m json.tool | head -60; echo ---; curl -s 'http://localhost:9274/ax-db-orders/_search' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"size":3}' | python3 -m json.tool | head -80
input.description: Get mapping and sample docs from ax-db-orders

input.command: curl -s 'http://localhost:9274/ax-db-orders/_count' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"query":{"exists":{"field":"amount_usd"}}}'; echo; curl -s 'http://localhost:9274/ax-db-orders/_count'; echo; curl -s 'http://localhost:9274/ax-db-orders/_search' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"size":0,"aggs":{"files":{"terms":{"field":"ax_file","size":20}},"paths":{"terms":{"field":"ax_path","size":20}},"tenants":{"terms":{"field":"tenant_id","size":20}}}}' | python3 -m json.tool
input.description: Count docs with amount_usd and check file/tenant breakdown

input.command: curl -s 'http://localhost:9274/ax-db-orders/_search' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"size":0,"aggs":{"min_id":{"min":{"field":"order_id"}},"max_id":{"max":{"field":"order_id"}},"uniq":{"cardinality":{"field":"order_id"}}}}'; echo; curl -s 'http://localhost:9274/ax-exports-csv-orders-march/_search' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"size":0,"aggs":{"min_id":{"min":{"field":"order_id"}},"max_id":{"max":{"field":"order_id"}},"uniq":{"cardinality":{"field":"order_id"}}}}'; echo; curl -s 'http://localhost:9274/ax-exports-csv-orders-march/_search' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"size":2}' | python3 -m json.tool | head -50
input.description: Compare order_id ranges between DB and CSV export

input.command: curl -s 'http://localhost:9274/autoindex-catalog/_search' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"size":30,"query":{"match":{"fields_json":"amount_usd"}},"_source":["slug","index_name","record_count"]}' | python3 -m json.tool; echo ---; curl -s 'http://localhost:9274/ax-db-orders/_search' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"query":{"term":{"order_id":500049}},"_source":["amount_usd","tenant_id","status"]}' | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['hits']['hits'])"
input.description: Find all datasets with amount_usd field; spot-check overlap

content.text: Only these two datasets contain `amount_usd`, and the spot-check confirms the CSV rows are exact duplicates of DB rows. So the complete, deduplicated order set is `ax-db-orders`. Now the per-tenant sum:

input.description: Sum amount_usd per tenant across all orders

content.text:
**t-northwind generated the most revenue: $2,576,038.83.**

Per-tenant totals across all 120,000 orders (sum of `amount_usd`):

| Tenant | Orders | Total amount (USD) |
|---|---:|---:|
| t-northwind | 23,422 | $2,576,038.83 |
| t-umbrella | 22,966 | $2,545,167.14 |
| t-stark | 22,124 | $2,474,356.62 |
| t-acme | 22,506 | $2,459,395.16 |
| t-globex | 21,350 | $2,375,772.85 |
| t-initech | 3,303 | $365,333.10 |
| t-hooli | 2,648 | $286,890.65 |
| t-wonka | 1,681 | $177,467.09 |
| **Grand total** | **120,000** | **$13,260,421.44** |

How I arrived at this:

- Order data with `amount_usd` exists in exactly two indices (confirmed via the `autoindex-catalog`): `ax-db-orders` (120,000 rows from `db/backup-2026-04-01.sql`, table `orders`) and `ax-exports-csv-orders-march` (50,000 rows from `exports/csv/orders_march.csv`).
- The CSV export is a **duplicate subset** of the DB backup: its order_ids (500000–549999) fall entirely inside the DB's range (500000–619999), the DB has 120,000 distinct order_ids with no gaps or dupes, and spot-checked orders match exactly (same amount, tenant, status) — the catalog's own cross-dataset analysis also reports 1.00 containment. So I summed only `ax-db-orders` to avoid double-counting the 50k exported orders.
- One caveat on interpretation: each row has a `currency` field (USD/EUR/GBP), but `amount_usd` is by name the USD-normalized amount, so I summed it directly. The totals also include all statuses (paid, pending, refunded, etc.); if you'd like revenue restricted to `status: paid` only, I can re-run the aggregation with that filter.
